skill.name: test-infrastructure
skill.description: Guides agents through auditing, writing, running, and maintaining meaningful automated tests for software projects.
skill.summary: Build real test coverage by finding gaps, writing assertions, and verifying behavior.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown fences, sample fetch calls, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are documentation examples for writing and running tests, not executable skill code or malicious instructions.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by markdown code fences and example blocks. The reviewed lines contain documentation, pseudocode, or sample tests, not executable Ruby backtick calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern matches markdown delimiters, and surrounding content is instructional text. No Ruby source file or executable backtick expression is present.

low_findings.title: Relative Fetch Examples Are Test Documentation
low_findings.description: The fetch calls are example integration tests against relative API routes. They do not send data to an external host and do not show credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both calls target '/api/contacts' inside sample test code. The examples use placeholder data and no external URL.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not reference weak cryptographic algorithms. They are YAML metadata, test suite names, or prose about testability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual context review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic primitive. The matches appear to come from ordinary words such as metadata and describe.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Test Guidance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss avoiding unnecessary mocks and testing missing authorization. They do not instruct host, user, network, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding sections are test-writing examples. No reconnaissance command, filesystem inventory, process listing, or secret discovery instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Software teams often have test files that do not prove important behavior. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find gaps, write meaningful tests, and verify coverage.
actual_capabilities: Inventories test files and separates real coverage from empty or shallow tests.
Prioritizes tests for critical paths such as authentication, API routes, database operations, and core workflows.
Guides creation of unit, integration, and component tests with real assertions.
Defines quality gates for tests that run, fail for real regressions, and remain maintainable.
Provides commands for running full suites, targeted suites, watch mode, and coverage reports.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include executable helper scripts.
Coverage targets are generic and must be adapted to each repository.
Example code assumes TypeScript, React, API routes, and npm-based test workflows.
It cannot prove that a new test catches bugs unless the agent mutates or breaks the implementation.

use_cases.title: Audit Existing Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Find empty tests, weak assertions, and missing coverage for critical product paths.

use_cases.title: Add Meaningful Regression Tests
use_cases.description: Turn known bugs and fragile flows into executable tests with clear assertions.

use_cases.title: Prepare CI Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Define practical test commands, coverage checks, and maintenance rules before release.

prompt_templates.title: Find Test Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-infrastructure skill to review this repository. Identify empty tests, shallow tests, and the top five missing critical-path tests.

prompt_templates.title: Write Critical Path Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-infrastructure skill to add real tests for the authentication flow. Include happy paths, failure paths, and authorization checks.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Integration Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-infrastructure skill to inspect API route coverage. Add integration tests that verify persistence, authorization, and error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Coverage Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-infrastructure skill to create a phased coverage plan. Prioritize revenue-critical paths, define commands, and include verification criteria.

output_examples.input: Review the test suite before launch.
output_examples.output: A prioritized coverage report listing real test coverage, empty files, critical gaps, estimated effort, and recommended next tests.

output_examples.input: Add tests for a new API endpoint.
output_examples.output: A set of integration test scenarios covering successful creation, invalid input, unauthorized access, persistence checks, and clear failure messages.

output_examples.input: Make our tests useful in CI.
output_examples.output: A quality gate plan with suite commands, coverage report guidance, flake checks, and maintenance metrics.

best_practices: Start with critical user and revenue paths before broad coverage targets.
Require each test to assert behavior that would fail if the feature broke.
Run tests after changes and verify that failures are clear and actionable.
anti_patterns: Counting empty test files as coverage.
Writing smoke tests that only check whether code renders or runs.
Mocking the main behavior that the test is supposed to verify.

faq.question: Does this skill write tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides a testing workflow for an AI agent to follow. The agent still needs repository context and permission to edit files.

faq.question: What kinds of tests does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes critical path, integration, component, unit, and edge case tests in that order.

faq.question: Can it work outside TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the testing principles transfer to other stacks. Some examples and commands must be adapted.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The fetch calls in the skill are local API test examples, not external network operations.

faq.question: Why does it warn against empty tests?
faq.answer: Empty tests can inflate coverage while failing to prove behavior. The skill asks for tests with real assertions.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found false positives in documentation examples and no confirmed malicious behavior.
